modules.name: lftk.lftk
documentation: -*- coding: UTF-8 -*-

functions.name: lftk.lftk.search_features
documentation:
Search features

Returns available linguistic features that match user-specified attributes. Putting "\*" on any attribute is analogous to "any". You can use this function to produce list of feature keys and pass them into LFTK Extractor's extract() function (see above).

# Parameters

domain

: A single domain name (e.g., "surface", "lexico-semantics")

family

: A single family name (e.g., "worddiff", "avgwordsent")

language

: A single supported language (e.g., "worddiff", "avgwordsent")

return_format

: Select how the searched features should be returned. The available options are "list_dict", "pandas", or "list_key".

# Returns

result

: Available features searched with user-given conditions

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import lftk
>>>
>>> output = lftk.search_features(domain = 'surface', family = "avgwordsent", language="general", return_format = "list_dict")
>>> # [{'key': 'a_word_ps', 'name': 'average_number_of_words_per_sentence', 'formulation': 'derivation', 'domain': 'surface', 'family': 'avgwordsent', 'language': 'general'}, {'key': 'a_char_ps', 'name': 'average_number_of_characters_per_sentence', 'formulation': 'derivation', 'domain': 'surface', 'family': 'avgwordsent', 'language': 'general'}, {'key': 'a_char_pw', 'name': 'average_number_of_characters_per_word', 'formulation': 'derivation', 'domain': 'surface', 'family': 'avgwordsent', 'language': 'general'}]
>>>
>>> output = lftk.search_features(domain = 'surface', family = "avgwordsent", language="general", return_format = "list_dict")
>>> #           key                                       name formulation   domain       family language
>>>  #9   a_word_ps       average_number_of_words_per_sentence  derivation  surface  avgwordsent  general
>>> # 10  a_char_ps  average_number_of_characters_per_sentence  derivation  surface  avgwordsent  general
>>> # 11  a_char_pw      average_number_of_characters_per_word  derivation  surface  avgwordsent  general
>>>
>>> output = lftk.search_features(domain = 'surface', family = "avgwordsent", language="general", return_format = "list_dict")
>>> # ['a_word_ps', 'a_char_ps', 'a_char_pw']
```

classes.name: lftk.lftk.Extractor

methods.name: lftk.lftk.Extractor.customize
documentation:
Global customization

Customizes all LFTK functions to extract features based on these options. This exclude some special functions that are intentionally designed to override global options.

# Parameters

stop_words

: Selection whether to include stop words for feature extraction

punctuations

: Selection whether to include punctuations for feature extraction

round_decimal

: The max number of decimal digits to return (for extracted feature values)

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import spacy
>>> import lftk
>>>
>>> nlp = spacy.load("en_core_web_sm")
>>> doc = nlp("I think effects computers have on people are great!")
>>>
>>> LFTK = lftk.Extractor(docs = doc)
>>>
>>> LFTK.customize(stop_words=True, punctuations=False, round_decimal=3)
>>> output = LFTK.extract(features = ["a_word_ps", "a_kup_pw", "n_noun"])
>>> # {'a_word_ps': 9.0, 'a_kup_pw': 5.323, 'n_noun': 3}
>>>
>>> LFTK.customize(stop_words=False, punctuations=False, round_decimal=2)
>>> output = LFTK.extract(features = ["a_word_ps", "a_kup_pw", "n_noun"])
>>> # {'a_word_ps': 5.0, 'a_kup_pw': 9.6, 'n_noun': 3}
```

methods.name: lftk.lftk.Extractor.extract
documentation:
Extract function for select features

Extracts feature(s) that are passed in by the user. All spaCy docs that were saved during LFTK Extractor declaration are used. This function extracts all features available in LFTK by default.

This function only accepts feature key(s) (e.g., "t_word", "a_kup_pw").

# Parameters

features

: A single feature key or a list of feature keys. Passing "\*" extracts all available features.

# Returns

result

: A dictionary or a list of dictionaries, depending on the number of spaCy docs you passed in during the creation of LFTK Extractor object.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import spacy
>>> import lftk
>>>
>>> nlp = spacy.load("en_core_web_sm")
>>> doc1 = nlp("I think effects computers have on people are great!")
>>> doc2 = nlp("I like drinking coffee...")
>>>
>>> LFTK = lftk.Extractor(docs = doc1)
>>>
>>> output = LFTK.extract(features = ["a_word_ps", "a_kup_pw", "n_noun"])
>>> # {'a_word_ps': 10.0, 'a_kup_pw': 4.791, 'n_noun': 3}
>>>
>>> LFTK = lftk.Extractor(docs = [doc1, doc2])
>>>
>>> output = LFTK.extract(features = ["a_word_ps", "a_kup_pw", "n_noun"])
>>> # [{'a_word_ps': 10.0, 'a_kup_pw': 4.791, 'n_noun': 3}, {'a_word_ps': 5.0, 'a_kup_pw': 2.284, 'n_noun': 2}]
```

Main object

Saves your spaCy doc objects to extract linguistic features from. As you declare this object, pass in spaCy doc(s).

# Parameters

docs

: spaCy doc object or a list of spacy doc objects

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import spacy
>>> import lftk
>>>
>>> nlp = spacy.load("en_core_web_sm")
>>> doc1 = nlp("I think effects computers have on people are great!")
>>> doc2 = nlp("I like drinking coffee...")
>>>
>>> LFTK = lftk.Extractor(docs = [doc1, doc2])
```

methods.name: lftk.lftk.SingleExtractor.__init__
documentation:
Initialize pipeline
input :
- self
- doc: single text inside spacy doc object
saves :
- self.doc: spacy doc object

methods.name: lftk.lftk.SingleExtractor.run
documentation:
Calculate linguistic feature(s) from spaCy doc
input :
- self
- features: features to be extracted
returns :
- result: extracted linguistic feature(s)

classes.name: lftk.derivation.readformula.ReadFormula

methods.name: lftk.derivation.readformula.ReadFormula.flesch_kincaid_reading_ease
documentation: returns reading difficulty value from flesch kincaid reading ease formula, where 100.00–90.00->5th grade (Very easy to read), 90.0–80.0->6th grade (Easy to read), 80.0–70.0->7th grade (Fairly easy to read), 70.0–60.0->8th & 9th grade (Plain English, 60.0–50.0->10th to 12th grade (Fairly difficult to read), 50.0–30.0->College (Difficult to read), 30.0–10.0->College graduate (Very difficult to read), 10.0–0.0->Professional (Extremely difficult to read)

methods.name: lftk.derivation.readformula.ReadFormula.flesch_kincaid_grade_level
documentation: returns reading difficulty value that corresponds to US grade level

methods.name: lftk.derivation.readformula.ReadFormula.gunning_fog_index
documentation: returns gunning fog index, where 17->College graduate, 16->College senior, 15->College junior, 14->College sophomore, 13->College freshman, 12->High school senior, 11->High school junior, 10->High school sophomore, 9->High school freshman, 8->Eighth grade, 7->Seventh grade, 6->Sixth grade

methods.name: lftk.derivation.readformula.ReadFormula.smog_index
documentation: returns reading difficulty value that corresponds to US grade level

methods.name: lftk.derivation.readformula.ReadFormula.coleman_liau_index
documentation: returns reading difficulty value that corresponds to US grade level

methods.name: lftk.derivation.readformula.ReadFormula.automated_readability_index
documentation: returns reading difficulty value that corresponds to US grade level

ReadFormula

Parent class for features that are in the 'readformula' family.

classes.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_per_word
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities / total number of words)

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_person_per_word
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are PERSON / total number of words) -> People, including fictional

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_norp_per_word
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are NORP / total number of words) -> Nationalities or religious or political groups

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_fac_per_word
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are FAC / total number of words) -> Buildings, airports, highways, bridges, etc.

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_org_per_word
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are ORG / total number of words) -> Companies, agencies, institutions, etc.

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_gpe_per_word
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are GPE / total number of words) -> Countries, cities, states.

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_loc_per_word
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are LOC / total number of words) -> Non-GPE locations, mountain ranges, bodies of water.

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_product_per_word
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are PRODUCT / total number of words) -> Objects, vehicles, foods, etc., but not services.

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_event_per_word
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are EVENT / total number of words) -> Named hurricanes, battles, wars, sports events, etc.

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_art_per_word
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are WORK_OF_ART / total number of words) -> Titles of books, songs, etc.

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_law_per_word
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are LAW / total number of words) -> Named documents made into laws.

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_language_per_word
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are LANGUAGE / total number of words) -> Any named language.

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_date_per_word
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are DATE / total number of words) -> Absolute or relative dates or periods.

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_time_per_word
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are TIME / total number of words) -> Times smaller than a day.

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_percent_per_word
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are PERCENT / total number of words) -> Percentage, including ”%“.

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_money_per_word
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are MONEY / total number of words) -> Monetary values, including unit.

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_quantity_per_word
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are QUANTITY / total number of words) -> Measurements, as of weight or distance.

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_ordinal_per_word
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are ORDINAL / total number of words) -> “first”, “second”, etc.

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_cardinal_per_word
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are CARDINAL / total number of words) -> Numerals that do not fall under another type.

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_per_sentence
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities / total number of sentences)

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_person_per_sentence

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_norp_per_sentence

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_event_per_sentence

documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are WORK_OF_ART / total number of sentences) -> Titles of books, songs, etc.

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_law_per_sentence
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are LAW / total number of sentences) -> Named documents made into laws.

documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are DATE / total number of sentences) -> Absolute or relative dates or periods.

documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are TIME / total number of sentences) -> Times smaller than a day.

documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are PERCENT / total number of sentences) -> Percentage, including ”%“.

methods.name: lftk.derivation.avgentity.AvgEntity.average_number_of_named_entities_money_per_sentence
documentation: returns the value of (total number of named entities that are MONEY / total number of sentences) -> Monetary values, including unit.
